title: Rental Housing
summary: There are over 49 million rental housing units in the United States. About one-third of households in the United States rent their housing. Rental housing prices have increased faster than prices for non-shelter goods and services in recent years. To address persistent housing affordability challenges, the federal government funds several rental housing assistance and production programs that either directly...

title: How Schools and After-School Programs Can Collaborate to Support Academic Alignment
summary: The authors provide national data about the state of academic alignment between school-day and after-school learning, illustrate how schools and after-school programs put academic alignment into practice, and identify enabling and impeding factors.

title: The Pacificus-Helvidius Debate Between Alexander Hamilton and James Madison
summary: Two men who were instrumental in the writing and ratification of the U.S. Constitution differed on how the document allocated power in foreign policy between the president and Congress.

title: Trump’s Iran Food Deal Could End Up Helping the Regime, Not Its People
summary: The Trump administration wants to release frozen Iranian funds—but only to buy U.S. farm goods. The proposal is less humanitarian gesture than market engineering, and without independent oversight, it risks repeating the catastrophic failures of the Iraq Oil-for-Food program.

title: USPS Releases Proposed Security, Transparency Standards for Mail-In Ballots
summary: If the U.S. Postal Service gets its way, some major changes are coming for mail-in ballots. It is a welcome prospect at a time when USPS budgets are shrinking and delivery times are getting longer. On June 2, USPS began a 30-day public comment period on its proposed rule to add security and transparency requirements for mail-in ballots. title: Victor Davis Hanson: Zohran Mamdani’s AIPAC Attack, Updates On The Iran Strategy, And America’s Urban Decline
summary: Hoover Institution fellow Victor Davis Hanson examines the Trump administration’s emerging agreement with Iran, arguing that the memorandum reflects a strategic effort to avoid a broader regional conflict.

title: The Declaration Of Independence | Michael Auslin
summary: Hoover Institution fellow Michael Auslin discusses the genesis of the Declaration of Independence and why the Declaration was and is so important.

title: Victor Davis Hanson: Why Foreign Adversaries Are Force-Reading 'The Art Of The Deal' To Figure Out Trump
summary: Hoover Institution fellow Victor Davis Hanson says while the left-wing media labels Donald Trump's foreign policy as chaotic, America's adversaries are secretly studying his business books just to survive his next move.

title: Can America Avoid A Jack Ma Moment?
summary: [Subscription Required] A globally recognizable tech executive, high-spirited from preparing for a public offering, offers imprudent remarks criticizing the government. The state strikes back harder than anyone expects. Overnight, the bargain between a skyrocketing sector of the economy and the government is shattered.

title: The Week AI Politics Went Mainstream
summary: This week's System Check: a whole range of establishment indicators show that the world is suddenly taking AI politics seriously---and discovering a bunch of Free Systems ideas along the way.

title: BNR Nieuws: China overspoelt Europa met goedkope export
summary: In deze aflevering van Boekestijn en De Wijk gaat presentator Hugo Reitsma in gesprek met Arend Jan Boekestijn, Rob de Wijk en econoom Sander Tordoir over de groeiende economische druk vanuit China. Volgens Tordoir beleeft de wereldeconomie een nieuwe 'China Shock 2.0'. Door honderden miljarden dollars aan subsidies, een ondergewaardeerde munt en een enorme exportgroei overspoelt China steeds meer buitenlandse...

title: The Temporary Protected Status program may effectively be over. Here's what we know.
summary: A Supreme Court ruling gives the Trump administration space to strip this status from hundreds of thousands of more people from the few remaining countries with this program.

title: Senate Ethics Committee dismisses complaint against Sen. Ruben Gallego
summary: The committee had been alerted by a fellow member of Congress of allegations of campaign finance violations and potential sexual misconduct, but said it found no evidence of wrongdoing.

title: Supreme Court cements Trump's power over agencies long considered independent
summary: In a 6-3 decision, the Supreme Court struck down a 91-year-old precedent that has prevented presidents from removing members of independent agencies meant to be a check on his power.

title: The Supreme Court upholds grace periods for mail-in ballots, siding against the GOP
summary: The U.S. Supreme Court has upheld a Mississippi law that allows election officials to count mail-in ballots that are postmarked by Election Day but received up to five days after it.

title: Red, white and glowing blue: Trump's push for new reactors reaches the finish line
summary: A program initiated by the Trump administration has allowed small companies to rush their testing of several new nuclear reactor designs. Some worry that safety is being compromised.

title: D.C. will pay $50,000 to man detained while protesting guard patrol with 'Star Wars' song, record says
summary: A settlement document reveals the District of Columbia has agreed to pay $50,000 to resolve a lawsuit filed by a resident who accused police officers of illegally detaining him for following an Ohio National Guard patrol while playing Darth Vader's theme song from "Star Wars" on his phone.

title: Supreme Court won't revive Alan Dershowitz's $300 million suit against CNN
summary: Dershowitz had urged the court to reconsider New York Times Co. v. Sullivan. The landmark First Amendment case that made it harder for public figures to win libel lawsuits because it requires proof that an outlet knowingly published something false, or showed a reckless disregard for the truth.

title: Supreme Court says Fed's Lisa Cook can keep her job for now, but it upholds other Trump firings
summary: The Supreme Court on Monday dramatically expanded presidential power, upholding President Donald Trump's firings of the heads of independent federal agencies with one important exception, the Federal Reserve.

title: Supreme Court rules states can count late-arriving mailed ballots, rejecting Trump-led challenge
summary: The decision rejected a Republican-led attack on laws in more than half the states and the District of Columbia that permit mailed ballots to arrive and be counted some number of days after the election, provided they are postmarked by Election Day.

title: Supreme Court will weigh Trump-backed Republican push to revive Arizona voting laws
summary: The Supreme Court said Monday it will consider a Republican push to enforce strict Arizona voting laws passed in the swing state after the 2020 election.

title: Justice Thomas makes appearance on Capitol Hill amid major rulings
summary: Supreme Court Justice Clarence Thomas was spotted Monday on Capitol Hill, amid the high court releasing a slate of opinions before it heads on recess for the summer. summary: President Trump slammed democratic socialist candidates Monday, calling their movement more dangerous to the U.S. than previous world wars and the Sept. 11, 2001, terrorist attacks. “They use the word social democrat because it sounds so nice, but it’s really communism you’re talking about,” Trump told reporters in the Oval Office. “I think it’s the...

title: Trump calls housing bill 'a yawn,' concedes SAVE America Act unlikely to pass
summary: President Trump referred to a bipartisan housing bill as a “yawn” on Monday, arguing that the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE America) Act is more important. title: Colorado high court tosses redistricting ballot measures
summary: The Colorado Supreme Court on Monday tossed out two cases involving new redistricting ballot measures that would have favored Democrats. One ruling said the redistricting measure violated the state constitution’s “single subject” requirement that calls for measures proposed by petition to focus on one issue for inclusion on the ballot. The Democratic aligned group, Coloradans...

title: Ethics Panel Tosses Misconduct Claims Against Gallego
summary: The Ethics Committee told Senator Ruben Gallego it had dismissed allegations he engaged in sexual misconduct and campaign finance violations.

title: Colorado Supreme Court Rejects Democratic Redistricting Plans
summary: The state’s high court found that ballot initiatives designed to change state law to install a newly gerrymandered map violated Colorado law.

title: Supreme Court Decision on Late-Arriving Mail-In Ballots Grants Relief to 18 States
summary: The Supreme Court ruled on Monday that state laws allowing ballots to arrive after Election Day are legal. The decision is the latest in a series of setbacks for President Trump’s efforts to regulate elections.

title: Fired F.T.C. Commissioner Warns of Potential for Presidential Abuse of Power
summary: Rebecca Slaughter said independent agencies like the Federal Trade Commission were created as watchdogs of powerful corporations and that presidents shouldn’t interfere with that.

title: In Dissents, Some Justices Push Back Against Ruling to Block Trump From Firing Fed Governor Lisa Cook
summary: A few dissenters cautioned that the majority didn’t allow the legal case over the president’s effort to fire the Fed governor Lisa D. Cook to play out in the lower courts.

title: Supreme Court expands Trump’s power over the federal bureaucracy
summary: The justices allowed the president to fire a Democratic member of the Federal Trade Commission, striking down a nearly century-old precedent intended to insulate independent agencies from political influence by the executive.

title: Supreme Court, for now, blocks Trump from firing Fed board member Lisa Cook
summary: The decision is a setback for Trump, who alleged that Cook committed mortgage fraud. She is the first Black woman to serve on the Fed board.

title: Housing bill that Trump refuses to sign heads to his desk
summary: House Speaker Mike Johnson is set to send a bipartisan housing bill to President Donald Trump’s desk Monday, but it is unclear whether he will sign it into law.

title: Supreme Court lets stand $5 million civil verdict against Trump in Carroll case
summary: A 2023 trial found President Donald Trump liable for allegedly sexually assaulting journalist E. Jean Carroll. The decision Monday leaves in place a ruling affirming the judgment.

title: Why resisting Trump has galvanized Black Democrats as the midterms approach
summary: High primary turnout among Black voters in the South has given some in the party hope for upset wins in the region.

title: Aftershock frays nerves as many Venezuelans left to fend for themselves
summary: In areas devastated by the twin earthquakes, people are using crowbars, pickaxes and their bare hands to try to reach survivors.

title: Mum rescued from Venezuela rubble with newborn baby tells BBC how he helped her survive
summary: The "miracle" rescue of 18-day-old baby Juan David is a symbol of hope in Venezuela after the devastating earthquakes.

title: Six people shot dead at centre for mothers and children in Germany
summary: The male suspect who has been arrested was in a custody dispute over his baby daughter, police say.

title: Trump's final appeal of E Jean Carroll sex abuse case rejected
summary: The Supreme Court decision means the US president will now have to pay Carroll the $5m (£3.6m) in damages she had been awarded.

title: US says it has agreed to 'stand down' after exchange of strikes with Iran
summary: A series of strikes over the weekend saw the US and Iran accuse each other of violating the ceasefire agreement.

title: Burnham vows to ‘rewire’ British state
summary: UK’s prime minister-in-waiting takes aim at centralisation and promises voters help on living costs

title: Supermicro Taiwan offices raided in chip smuggling probe
summary: Server maker’s shares fell about 8% after news of the investigation

summary: European commissioner Maroš Šefčovič demands action after meeting Chinese commerce minister Wang Wentao

summary: President of central bank signals that there could be a modest increase in interest rates

title: President Harry Truman’s 1950 Decision to Intervene in Korea
summary: Truman’s decision not to seek congressional authorization for his decision to commit U.S. troops to defend South Korea set a precedent that continues to shape the war powers today.

title: Identifying Minors Online
summary: Concerns about potential harms to minors on the internet, particularly on social media platforms, have grown in recent years. Dating to the 1990s, policymakers have enacted legislation seeking to protect minors online, some of which create requirements for entities that provide websites, mobile applications, and online platforms (collectively, website operators). Website operators have developed various methods to...

title: Cybersecurity: Considerations on Regulatory Harmonization
summary: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is simultaneously pursuing three major cybersecurity regulatory initiatives—each issued by a different agency, each addressing a different critical infrastructure sector, and each with its own definitions, timelines, and reporting requirements for cybersecurity incidents. Some stakeholders have raised concerns about the potential burdens that the three separate, but...

title: What Is Temporary Protected Status?
summary: Temporary Protected Status (TPS) has long been used as a humanitarian solution for migrants who are unable to return home safely. President Trump’s efforts to scale back such programs have further fueled the debate over the immigration policy’s use and scope.

title: Myths, Fables, and Hard Truths About AI Governance
summary: CFR President Michael Froman assesses the U.S. government’s de facto ban on Anthropic’s most powerful AI model and what it reveals about the U.S. regulatory framework for AI.

title: Against Caesar’s Cost of Glory
summary: Worries that the American republic is at the end of the line invite speculation on what the future has in store. Some have speculated that perhaps Caesarism is in our future. Caesarism stands for the proposition that our current republican system, and especially our legislature, either from corruption or factionalism, is no longer up to the task of governing an empire. The more far-flung our empire, the more it...

title: Have Rising Living Standards Pushed Marriage out of Reach?
summary: Have rising material expectations made marriage feel less attainable for today’s young adults? Marriage rates have steadily been declining for several decades. Roughly one-third of Gen Z is expected to remain single by age 45, and to perhaps never marry at all. A common reason young adults give for not being married is a lack of financial resources. And a popular narrative these days says that declining wages among...

title: Children Deserve Education, Not Gender Ideology
summary: From coast to coast, states such as California and New Jersey face no shortage of serious challenges, from the soaring cost of living and rising crime to homelessness and drug abuse. Yet one issue that often escapes the spotlight is the ideological agenda increasingly being promoted in K-12 classrooms. Having attended middle schools in these states, we’ve witnessed these lessons in health classes, where, at age 12,...

title: Swiss Miss Out on Chance To Save Their Nation From Immigrant Invasion
summary: Snow caps, yes: people caps, no. In a national referendum this week, the people of Switzerland voted against a hard cap of 10 million on the population. The vote was called by the conservative Swiss People’s Party in an effort to control the country’s population growth, which is driven entirely by immigration. Switzerland is a unicorn among the world’s nations: a successful multilingual state. The country was...

title: The U.S. Marine Corps Marine Littoral Regiment (MLR)
summary: Background In March 2020, the U.S. Marine Corps (USMC) announced a major force design initiative planned to occur over 10 years originally referred to as “Force Design 2030” which is now known as “Force Design.” Under Force Design, the Marines are redesigning forces to place a stronger emphasis on naval expeditionary warfare. As part of the redesign, the Marines originally planned to establish at least three Marine...

title: Supply Chain, Energy, and AI Nexus
summary: The authors identify the inventory of critical electrical equipment for artificial intelligence (AI) data center operations and the impact of supply chain vulnerabilities on the U.S. power grid’s ability to meet AI demand by 2030.

title: Can LLM Agents Select and Engage with Biological Tools?
summary: Large language model agents are capable of performing initial interactions with biological tools (BTs), indicating that agents could lower expertise barriers for actors seeking to use BTs. Targeted testing of agent design capabilities is warranted.

title: AI agents put offensive cyber within reach of novices
summary: Agentic AI models now enable non-experts to conduct advanced offensive cyberattacks. This study finds AI agents can autonomously breach complex systems rapidly, highlighting urgent needs for evolved defence and better cyber risk assessments.

title: Understanding How Impact Fees Shape Housing Development Feasibility and Local Fiscal Revenue
summary: High impact fees contribute meaningfully to housing production costs in California. A 25-percent reduction enables hundreds of new projects in high-cost cities; increased property and sales tax revenue offsets forgone fees in four to seven years.

title: Maternal Mortality Trends in the United States: Data in Brief
summary: Maternal mortality is considered a sentinel indicator of a nation’s health – a measure that can signal the health status of women, children, and families, as well as the progress and quality of the broader health system. Globally, maternal deaths have substantially decreased over the past three decades; however, the United States is among a handful of countries where maternal deaths have either increased, or...

title: Unpacking Europe: The path to EU enlargement
summary: On this week’s Unpacking Europe podcast Zselyke Csaky sat down with Milan Nic a senior research fellow at the German Council on Foreign Relations (DGAP) to discuss the latest on EU enlargement. They took a detailed look at new and old ideas recently put forward, including a so-called associate membership for Ukraine, as well as at progress by candidate countries. summary: Armida van Rij joined Quentin Peel to assess Germany's Friedrich Merz one year into his chancellorship on The Monocle Daily. Armida van Rij View multimedia 07 May 2026
